title: Incorrectly Managed Heap Size in Priority Queue

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, build a priority queue where the smallest element is always extracted first. However, the priority queue's internal heap size management is incorrect, leading to incorrect results when the heap is full and a new element is added. Fix the code to maintain the correct internal heap size.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a space-separated list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be the smallest element in the priority queue for each input.
statement.notes: Time complexity expectation: O(n * log n), where n is the size of the array. Space complexity expectation: O(n).

examples.explanation: The priority queue stores the smallest elements first. When the heap is full and a new element (1) is added, the existing heap should be updated to maintain the correct internal heap size.

hints.text: Check the internal heap size management in the priority queue implementation.
Verify that the priority queue is maintaining the correct heap size after each operation.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: A priority queue is implemented using a min heap. When adding a new element, check if the heap size exceeds the capacity. If it does, remove the maximum element (the root of the max heap) to maintain the correct internal heap size.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute-force approach is to rebuild the heap every time an element is added or removed, which leads to O(n) time complexity for simple operations.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a binary heap data structure, which supports efficient insertion and removal of elements while maintaining the heap property.
editorial.optimalSolution: In the optimal solution, we maintain a separate max heap to track the root of the min heap. When adding a new element, we check if the heap size is full. If it is, we remove the maximum element from the max heap to maintain the correct internal heap size.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution ensures that the priority queue maintains the correct internal heap size after each operation, resulting in the correct output.
editorial.dryRun: Let's dry-run the priority queue operations to verify the correctness of the optimal solution.
